title: Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical remote-sensing literature for this principle.

E.description: Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR): subsurface em radar produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.emit.em_pulse -> L.subsurface_reflect -> L.migration -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a non-convex inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D subsurface dielectric. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~15; calibration-level mismatch (soil_permittivity_variation, clutter, attenuation_depth) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y(t, x) = sum_z reflectivity(x, z) * pulse(t - 2 * traveltime(x,z)) + clutter + n; Kirchhoff or reverse-time migration for recovery
E.world_state_x: Scene-side quantity of interest (reflectance / velocity / phase / concentration)
E.observation_y: Sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D subsurface dielectric is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is local rather than global (non-convex landscape); convergence depends on initialisation and priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 15); soil_permittivity_variation dominates the stability cliff; clutter and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Kirchhoff-Migration, Reverse-Time-Migration] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [GPR-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |soil_permittivity_variation| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
